data.narrative:
Blue Origin, a private Space & Aerospace company founded by Jeff Bezos, carries a stated valuation of $30B+ against $10B+ in total capital raised. The peer universe for valuation purposes comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including Relativity Space and SpaceX, though the latter command valuations at materially different scales.

Peer-median triangulation is the applicable methodology here. A filtered set of 3 same-sector comparables at similar valuation tiers—Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), and Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B)—yields a peer median of $7.0B, with a range of $6.3B to $37.0B. Public-comp anchoring via Price-to-Sales multiples cannot be applied; while the Space & Aerospace sector median stands at 59.8x across 6 public tickers, Blue Origin does not disclose revenue, precluding this approach for private companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $6.0B to $8.0B, centered on a $7.0B median, based on the single applicable method. This range sits substantially below the stated $30B+ valuation. Key uncertainties include the maturity of Blue Origin's commercial revenue streams, capital efficiency relative to peers, and whether the private peer set—particularly SpaceX—reflects fundamentally different value drivers not captured by public-market comparables.
